source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Find the area of rectilinear shapes by counting squares.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack introduces area as covered space, then connects counting squares to rectangle arrays.

objective.child_goal: I can find the area of a rectangle using square units.
objective.statement: Find and explain the area of rectangles and simple rectilinear shapes using square units and array structure.
misconceptions: Confuses area with perimeter by counting around the edge.
Counts grid lines instead of square units.
Uses length plus width instead of rows times columns for rectangles.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The amount of flat space a shape covers.
vocabulary.audio_script: Area is the space covered inside the shape.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A rectangle filled with glowing squares.

vocabulary.child_explanation: One equal square used to measure area.
vocabulary.audio_script: Each little square is one square unit.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The distance around the edge of a shape.
vocabulary.audio_script: Perimeter is around the outside, not the inside.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A line tracing the outside edge.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Tap the inside space of the shape.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Separate area from perimeter before measuring.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Area is the inside space. Perimeter is around the edge.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Inside fill and outside edge toggle on a rectangle.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Cover the floor with equal square tiles.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce area as counting square units.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: To measure area, cover the space with equal squares.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A workshop floor fills with square tiles.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: A 4 by 5 rectangle has 4 rows of 5 squares.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect counting squares to array multiplication.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Four rows of five square units makes twenty square units.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Rows and columns highlight on a 4 by 5 grid.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Fill the rectangle and count the square units.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise complete tiling without gaps or overlaps.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Learner drags square tiles into a rectangular grid.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which answer is area, not perimeter?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Area counts inside squares, not the outside edge.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Inside tiles and outside border compete as two answer paths.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Rex why length plus width is not the area.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I added the length and width. Why did I miss some squares?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion counts only one row and one column before repair.

manipulatives.purpose: Represent area as covered square units and connect rectangular grids to multiplication.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners fill grids, highlight rows/columns, compare area and perimeter, and explain square-unit reasoning.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard fill controls, high-contrast grid lines and reduced-motion static fills.

manipulatives.purpose: Contrast area with perimeter by making learners choose whether a habitat design question concerns floor tiles or boundary fencing.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners inspect rectangular creature habitats, activate floor or fence tools, choose the matching calculation and compare designs that share an area but have different perimeters.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard shortcuts and large named buttons select floor, fence and calculation choices; single-switch scanning follows a fixed tool-answer-check order; static mode uses labelled rectangle diagrams with separate inside and boundary data; reduced-motion mode replaces tool sweeps with instant patterned floor fills or thick fence outlines and text summaries.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave perimeter only after area as square units is stable.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Distinguish inside area from outside perimeter.
Count complete square units in rectangles.
Use rows times columns to find rectangle area.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: length x width grid + multiplication link

variant_blueprints.purpose: Find area of simple rectilinear shapes by decomposing into rectangles.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: L-shape grid + split line + count regions

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain area/perimeter distinction and rectangle area facts.

body.prompt: Find the area of a rectangle with 4 rows of 5 square units.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Count rows of equal squares.
4 rows of 5 is 4 x 5.
question_variants.explanation: The area is 20 square units because 4 x 5 = 20.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 20. The area is 20 square units because 4 x 5 = 20.
feedback.repair: Count rows of equal squares. 4 rows of 5 is 4 x 5. Use that clue to repair length plus width, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this array build, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 20: The area is 20 square units because 4 x 5 = 20.
feedback.misconception_check: length plus width
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Which measurement tells the inside space covered by a shape?
choices: area
perimeter
height
mass
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: area

hints: Inside space means covered space.
Perimeter is around the edge.
question_variants.explanation: Area measures the inside space a shape covers.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "area". Area measures the inside space a shape covers.
feedback.repair: Inside space means covered space. Perimeter is around the edge. Use that clue to repair area perimeter confusion, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this tap choice, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "area": Area measures the inside space a shape covers.
feedback.misconception_check: area perimeter confusion
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: A rectangle is 3 squares by 6 squares. What is its area?
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Count square spaces, not grid lines.
question_variants.explanation: The area is 18 square units.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 18. The area is 18 square units.
feedback.repair: Count square spaces, not grid lines. 3 x 6 = 18. Use that clue to repair counts grid lines, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this grid fill, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 18: The area is 18 square units.
feedback.misconception_check: counts grid lines
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: A designer wants to cover the inside of the seed tray. Which measurement is needed?
choices: area
perimeter
length only
mass

manipulative_routes.virtual: equal square tiles with add, remove, row-highlight and fill-all buttons
manipulative_routes.concrete: adult-prepared square tiles on a bordered grid mat
manipulative_routes.drawn: squared paper with rows, columns and non-overlapping regions labelled
manipulative_routes.symbolic: rows × columns or sum/subtraction of rectangular areas

send_routes.visual: high-contrast square spaces, patterned row bands and persistent dimension labels; colour is never required
send_routes.concrete: large tactile square tiles and raised boundary or Wikki Stix outline
send_routes.text: short numbered dimensions, area table and calculation sentence
send_routes.oral: adult or partner reads dimensions and records the learner's explanation

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses area with perimeter by counting around the edge.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses edge count when asked for area.
repair_sequence: Ask learner to choose inside or edge for a new shape.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects and measures inside square units.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses length plus width instead of rows times columns for rectangles.
repair_sequence: Show each row has the same number of squares.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner uses rows times columns on a new rectangle.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm grid fills and no flashing tiles.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use instant tile fill and static row highlights.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Audio is not required to understand these spatial models. Use visible dimensions, tactile tiles, concise text or adult/partner reading. Browser TTS is prohibited; any future narration must use a human-reviewed ElevenLabs asset and retain the complete non-audio route.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use labelled rows, columns and square units.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Separate fill, count and multiply steps.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow tile recount and row/column toggles.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Treat perimeter answers as a useful comparison to repair.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach area, perimeter, inside, edge, row, column and square unit.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires counting square units and finding rectangle area; secure requires explaining area/perimeter distinction and array structure.
evidence.parent_summary: Can find the area of rectangles using square units and explain that area is inside space.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Counts square units, uses rows and columns, and avoids perimeter confusion.
next_actions: Interleave with perimeter after area is secure.
Use area models to support multiplication.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 4 area pack with a deterministic 240-variant pilot bank and three preserved curated variants. Coverage progresses from inside-versus-edge meaning and counting square units through rectangular arrays, length-times-width reasoning, missing dimensions, comparison and equivalence, simple rectilinear decomposition, units, misconception repair and transfer. Generated variants provide tile, grid, equation, concrete and reduced-load routes; keyboard, switch, touch and non-drag controls; rich feedback; and untimed workshop missions. Audio is unnecessary for generated items, browser TTS is prohibited, and any future ElevenLabs asset requires human review. Independent mathematics, teacher, accessibility, safeguarding and renderer review remain required before promotion.
